title: Architecture-Specific Schedules
research_question: Does the current hybrid-mixer frontier want a different training tail, especially near quantization and export, than the old baseline schedule?

candidates.name: r1-mild-warmdown
candidates.goal: Test whether the hybrid winner wants a slightly gentler global tail.
candidates.hypothesis: A short mild warmdown may leave the compressed model in a better final state without freezing learning too early.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the winner wants any global tail change at all.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest first schedule probe on the new best architecture.
candidates.description: add a short mild warmdown to the hybrid winner

candidates.name: r2-longer-warmdown
candidates.goal: Test whether the hybrid winner wants a much calmer finish.
candidates.hypothesis: The new backbone may be more quantization-sensitive than the old baseline and may benefit from a longer tail.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the global-tail question is directional or not.
candidates.why_worthy: This brackets R1 instead of assuming one warmdown level.
candidates.description: add a longer warmdown to the hybrid winner

candidates.name: r3-final-stabilize
candidates.goal: Test whether a brief export-focused cooldown is better than a broad warmdown.
candidates.hypothesis: The right intervention may be a short stabilization phase near the end rather than a colder tail over many steps.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether final settling helps independently of warmdown.
candidates.why_worthy: This isolates stabilization from broad schedule changes.
candidates.description: add only a short final stabilization phase

candidates.name: r4-head-focused-cooldown
candidates.goal: Test whether the output path remains the fragile part even on the new hybrid frontier.
candidates.hypothesis: The compressed model may improve if the head cools differently from the backbone near the end.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the quantization-sensitive surface is still mostly in the output path.
candidates.why_worthy: Earlier quantization results hinted this was the only near-miss schedule direction.
candidates.description: cool the head differently from the backbone at the end

candidates.name: r5-mild-tail-plus-head-cooldown
candidates.goal: Test the strongest justified schedule combo on the new architecture.
candidates.hypothesis: The best tail may need both a mild global decay and extra control over the output path.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the useful schedule effect is additive or singular.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the one schedule combo that follows directly from the single-factor bracket.
candidates.description: combine a mild warmdown with head-focused final cooldown
